summary: Closed, unmerged tooling cleanup PR that proposed deleting tools/Makefile, an 8-line standalone makefile for building elf2dol. The only recorded feedback objected that there was no clear reason to remove a makefile that lets elf2dol be built independently.
what_changed: Removed the standalone elf2dol build rule that compiled elf2dol.c using CC with CFLAGS set to -O3 -Wall -s.
Removed the clean target that deleted the elf2dol binary.
smart_moves: The proposed diff was narrowly scoped to a single tooling file with no source or decompilation changes.
The PR title clearly stated the author's rationale: the makefile was considered vestigial and completely unused.
decomp_lessons: Do not remove small standalone tooling build paths solely because they appear unused; they may still serve a useful independent-build workflow.
For build-system cleanup, provide evidence that the file is actually obsolete or superseded, especially when deleting a simple tool-specific makefile.
Closed/unmerged PRs can encode negative guidance: preserving low-cost utility files may be preferable to aggressive cleanup.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: I don't see any reason to remove a makefile for building elf2dol on its own.
review_feedback.impact: The PR was closed without being merged, suggesting the deletion rationale was not accepted based on the available context.
